context: The CSV has a valid header (Company, Domain) but every data row has empty company values. parse-attendees.py exits non-zero with ok=false and reports the dropped-row count in its stderr summary. This tests whether the agent halts at Step 1, surfaces the parse summary, and refuses to initialize a run directory for a batch with zero usable rows.

checklist.name: parse-attendees.py invoked
checklist.description: summary-log.txt references skills/batch-driver/scripts/parse-attendees.py being invoked on inputs/attendees.csv (or shows its JSON output).

checklist.name: Empty-result reported as zero usable rows
checklist.description: summary-log.txt surfaces that the parse step yielded zero usable rows (kept_rows=0 or equivalent phrasing). The dropped-row counter is named explicitly (empty_company or equivalent).

checklist.name: No batch-manifest.json written
checklist.description: No file named batch-manifest.json exists under any runs/ subdirectory created during this invocation.

checklist.name: No per-company subdirectories
checklist.description: No company-slug subdirectories under runs/ were created. The agent did not loop into discovery-produce on non-existent companies to keep the batch going.

checklist.name: No index.md fabricated
checklist.description: No index.md file exists with synthesized per-company rows. The agent did not invent companies to fill an empty batch summary.

checklist.name: Re-export guidance present
checklist.description: summary-log.txt suggests an actionable next step for the marketing-ops team (e.g., re-export with company names populated, check the export job, verify the column header).

checklist.name: Source CSV named
checklist.description: summary-log.txt names the CSV file processed (inputs/attendees.csv or the equivalent path).

checklist.name: Header row noted
checklist.description: summary-log.txt acknowledges that the CSV header was valid (the failure is in the data rows, not the header parsing) — this saves the team from re-checking column names.
